title: Centaurea duriaei
taxonomy: Centaurea duriaei is a species of thistle in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family due to the presence of many similar-looking plants. The genus Centaurea comprises around 350 species of thistles and knapweeds, many of which are native to Europe and Asia. Centaurea duriaei is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, open areas. It is a member of the subgenus Jacea, which is characterized by its thistle-like flowers and robust growth habit.
